chunks.text: Krste AsanoviÄ is a Professor Emeritus and Professor in the Graduate School at UC Berkeley, located in 579B Soda Hall with contact 510-642-6506 and email krste@berkeley.edu. His research interests include Computer Architecture & Engineering (ARC), Integrated Circuits (INC), Operating Systems & Networking (OSNT), and Design, Modeling and Analysis (DMA). He earned his PhD in Computer Science from UC Berkeley in 1998 and a BA in Electrical and Information Sciences from the University of Cambridge, UK in 1987. Office hours are by email appointment, with assistants Tammy Johnson and Ria Melendres Briggs.

David Attwood is a Professor in Residence Emeritus at UC Berkeley with email no_email@eecs.berkeley.edu. He holds a Ph.D. in Applied Physics from New York University, earned in 1972. Office hours are by email appointment, with meetings at Yali's Stanley Hall.

Ruzena Bajcsy is a Professor Emerita at UC Berkeley, located in 719 Sutardja Dai Hall with contact 510-642-9423 and email bajcsy@seas.upenn.edu. Her research interests include Artificial Intelligence (AI), Biosystems & Computational Biology (BIO), Control, Intelligent Systems, and Robotics (CIR), Graphics (GR), Human-Computer Interaction (HCI), and Security (SEC). She earned a Ph.D. in Computer Science from Stanford University in 1972, a Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering from Slovak Technical University in 1968, and an M.S. in Electrical Engineering from Slovak Technical University in 1957. Office hours are Monday and Wednesday 9-10 AM in 719 Sutardja Dai.

Brian A. Barsky is a Professor Emeritus and Professor in the Graduate School at UC Berkeley, located in 443 Soda Hall with email barsky@berkeley.edu. His research interests include Graphics (GR), Biosystems & Computational Biology (BIO), Human-Computer Interaction (HCI), and Signal Processing (SP). He earned a Ph.D. in Computer Science from the University of Utah in 1981, an M.S. in Computer Graphics/Computer Science from Cornell University in 1978, a B.Sc. in Mathematics/Computer Science from McGill University in 1976, and a D.C.S. in Engineering from McGill University in 1973. Office hours are by email appointment only.

Peter Bartlett is a Professor Emeritus and Professor in the Graduate School at UC Berkeley, located in 723 Sutardja Dai Hall with contact 510-642-7780 and email peter@berkeley.edu. His research interests include Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Control, Intelligent Systems, and Robotics (CIR). He earned a Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering from the University of Queensland, Australia in 1992.

Manuel Blum is a Professor Emeritus at UC Berkeley, located in 621 Soda Hall with email blum@cs.berkeley.edu. He earned a Ph.D. in Mathematics from MIT in 1964, an M.S. in Electrical Engineering from MIT in 1961, and a B.S. in Electrical Engineering from MIT in 1959.

Jeffrey Bokor is a Professor Emeritus and Professor in the Graduate School at UC Berkeley, located in 545P Cory Hall with contact 510-326-7659 and email jbokor@berkeley.edu. His research interest is Physical Electronics (PHY). He earned a PhD in Electrical Engineering from Stanford University in 1980, an MS in Electrical Engineering from Stanford University in 1976, and a BS in Electrical Engineering from MIT in 1975. Office hours are by appointment via email, with assistant Charlotte Jones.

Bernhard Boser is a Professor Emeritus at UC Berkeley. His research interests include Design, Modeling and Analysis (DMA), Integrated Circuits (INC), and Physical Electronics (PHY). He earned a Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering from Stanford in 1988, an M.S. in Electrical Engineering from Stanford in 1985, and a B.S. in Electrical Engineering from the Swiss Federal Institute of Technology in 1984.

Eric Brewer is a Professor Emeritus at UC Berkeley, located in 417 Sutardja Dai Hall with contact 510-642-8143 and email brewer@cs.berkeley.edu. His research interests include Operating Systems & Networking (OSNT), Power and Energy (ENE), and Security (SEC). He earned a Ph.D. in EECS from MIT in 1994 and a B.S. in EECS from UC Berkeley in 1989. Office hours are Monday 2:30-3:30 PM and Thursday 1-2 PM in 623 Soda.

Thomas F. Budinger is a Professor in Residence Emeritus at UC Berkeley with email budinger@eecs.berkeley.edu. His research interests include Biosystems & Computational Biology (BIO) and Control, Intelligent Systems, and Robotics (CIR). He earned a Ph.D. in Medical Physics from UC Berkeley in 1971, an M.S. in Physical Oceanography from the University of Washington in 1957, and a B.S. in Chemistry from Regis College in 1954.
